title: Intensely Savory Canadian Fregola Pilaf
description: A briny and dense Canadian dish featuring fregola, pickle, and ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly ingredients, served in a bamboo tray. This midnight-craving dish is perfect for a savory snack.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the toasted fregola with olive oil, salt, and black pepper until the fregola is well coated.
In a large skillet, saute the chopped onion and minced garlic over medium heat until the onion is translucent.
Add the pickle juice, dried thyme, and cooked fregola to the skillet and stir to combine.
Transfer the fregola mixture to a bamboo tray and bake in the preheated oven for 15 minutes.
Remove the tray from the oven and stir in the chopped fresh parsley.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Let the pilaf cool to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing.
Reheat the pilaf in the oven or microwave until warmed through.
Refrigerate and consume within 1 week — this preparation is not shelf-stable without an approved acid level and canning process.

Refrigerator pickle only: store in the fridge and consume within 1 week. For shelf-stable canning, add at least 2 tablespoons of vinegar (5% acidity) per cup of brine and follow a tested water-bath process.
